objections.description: Mobile command palette has two triggers (bottom-nav + floating). This creates interaction ambiguity and wastes critical viewport space on small devices. Contradicts mobile-first simplification principles.
objections.suggested_fix: Remove floating trigger on document routes. Retain only bottom-nav trigger to preserve reading space and reduce cognitive load.

objections.description: Second-hop query is implemented but lacks visualization. Fetching data without an interactive traversal mechanism is backend work masquerading as frontend value. Users cannot explore relationships.
objections.suggested_fix: Implement force-directed graph or expandable list view for second-hop nodes. Enable click-to-navigate for related documents.

objections.description: Legal status confidence scores are decoupled from specific text citations. Rendering 'model evidence' without direct anchoring to document ranges creates liability risk for professional research.
objections.suggested_fix: Enforce strict citation linking. Each confidence score must reference byte offsets or paragraph IDs in the source document.

suggestions.description: Add keyboard shortcut hinting for desktop users to reduce reliance on mouse traversal.
Add timestamp versioning to status objects to track legal status changes over time explicitly.
